ogTitle: Boomerang Beach vs Boomey: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Boomerang Beach and Boomey.
heading: Boomerang Beach vs Boomey — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Boomerang Beach, New South Wales and Boomey, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Boomey has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Boomerang Beach (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Boomerang Beach or Boomey safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Boomerang Beach has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Boomey scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Boomerang Beach scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Boomey scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Boomerang Beach or Boomey?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Boomey has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
